response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine developer-tooling credentials — including hands-on work on an actual IDE product (NI LabWindows/CVI) and authoring test runners and assertion libraries — but the core stack requirements are not met. His depth is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang; Java appears only peripherally, and there is no evidence of Kotlin, Spring, or deep JVM expertise, which are hard requirements for building enterprise Java/Kotlin tooling. An unexplained employment gap in 2020 is a minor additional question mark.
strengths: Direct IDE product experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI)
Authentic language-tooling interest: built test runners, assertion libraries, and web servers from scratch
Long track record across complex, high-throughput systems (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin or Spring ecosystem experience, and only peripheral Java work
Primary expertise (TypeScript/Node.js, DLang) does not match the JVM-centric technical focus
Unexplained ~8-month employment gap between January 2020 and September 2020
